claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child, I need to ask you some questions.

First, **what is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to understand their current situation better.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (Child Benefit can normally continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but it typically stops once they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They're actually working full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Decision for Alex:**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

- Child Benefit is available for children **under 16**, or for young people **under 20** if they stay in approved education or training
- Alex is **21 years old**, which means they are beyond the maximum age limit for Child Benefit
- Additionally, since Alex is working full-time, they would not qualify under the education/training provision even if they were under 20

Child Benefit stops when a child turns 20, even if they are still in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and working full-time, you are not entitled to receive Child Benefit for them.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they exceed the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or employment status. Additionally, Alex is working full-time, which would also make them ineligible even if they were in the relevant age bracket. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. Child Benefit eligibility requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they are beyond the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they exceed the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or employment status. Additionally, Alex is working full-time, which would also make them ineligible even if they were in the relevant age bracket. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Child Benefit eligibility requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 and in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they are beyond the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
